regions.us-ports: Today in Miami I am reporting ESE 5kt winds, an overcast sky and less than 10% rain probability with thunderstorms on the daily forecast, and I am flagging Miami, Port Everglades and Port Canaveral as the ports to monitor. For embarkation and shore excursions today I am using the Day 1 forecast of ESE 10kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability and the overcast sky to sequence gangway operations and outdoor activity routing at Miami and Port Everglades. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Day 2 calls for E 15kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, so I am watching Port Canaveral and Tampa Bay for the stronger easterly flow that may require adjustments to tendering or berth assignments. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows ESE 11kt with thunderstorms and 10% rain probability, and I will continue tracking that ESE 11kt trend for turnarounds and shore excursion planning at Miami and Port Everglades.
